# Braintrust AWS Data Breach Prompts Urgent API Key Rotation for AI Platform Customers

![Braintrust AWS Data Breach Prompts Urgent API Key Rotation for AI Platform Customers](https://www.rescana.com/post/braintrust-aws-data-breach-prompts-urgent-api-key-rotation-for-ai-platform-customers/cover.png)

## **Executive Summary**

On May 4, 2026, **Braintrust**, an AI evaluation platform, detected unauthorized access to one of its **Amazon Web Services (AWS)** cloud accounts containing customer API keys. The company immediately locked down the affected account, restricted access to related systems, and rotated internal credentials. Customers were notified on May 5, 2026, and advised to revoke and regenerate any API keys stored with **Braintrust**. As of the latest verified reports, only one customer has been confirmed as directly affected, though three additional customers reported suspicious spikes in AI provider usage, which are under investigation. There is no evidence of broader exposure or data exfiltration at this time. The incident highlights the risks associated with storing sensitive credentials in third-party AI platforms and underscores the importance of robust cloud security and supply chain risk management. The cause of the breach remains under investigation, and **Braintrust** is implementing additional safeguards to prevent recurrence.

## **Technical Information**

The incident involved unauthorized access to a **Braintrust** AWS account that stored API keys used by customers to access cloud-based AI models. The initial access vector has not been publicly disclosed, but the compromise of cloud credentials is strongly indicated by the company’s incident response actions and public statements. Upon discovery of suspicious activity on May 4, 2026, **Braintrust** immediately locked down the compromised AWS account, audited and restricted access across related systems, and rotated all internal secrets. Customers were promptly notified and provided with indicators of compromise and remediation guidance.

The attack method aligns with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1078 (Valid Accounts), specifically T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts), where attackers use stolen or compromised credentials to access cloud resources as legitimate users. This technique is commonly employed by both financially motivated and state-sponsored threat actors, though no specific attribution has been made in this case. There is no evidence of malware deployment, persistence mechanisms, or unique threat actor tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) in the current incident.

# AWS targets AI agent payments

Amazon Web Services has teamed up with Stripe and Coinbase to enable AI agents to autonomously access and pay for MCP servers, web content, and other agents.

1 Like014 hours [Be the first to comment](https://www.finextra.com/newsarticle/47731/aws-targets-ai-agent-payments#comments)

![AWS targets AI agent payments](https://www.finextra.com/finextra-images/top_pics/xxl/aws-logo.png)

#### Editorial

This content has been selected, created and edited by the Finextra editorial team based upon its relevance and interest to our community.

AWS claims that Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments marks the first managed payment capabilities purpose-built for autonomous agents, spanning the full lifecycle from wallet authentication through transaction execution to spending governance and observability.

Coinbase and Stripe are providing the wallet infrastructure and payment rails that power the first set of capabilities.

Developers can connect their agent to a wallet or payment service provider, register a funded payment source, and set spending limits per session.

AgentCore manages credential authentication and token lifecycle. When the agent encounters a paid resource during execution, AgentCore handles protocol negotiation, retries, and payment, routing the transaction through the appropriate provider without interrupting the agent’s reasoning loop.

Says AWS: "A financial research agent can dynamically access real-time market data feeds and paywalled publications, paying for the articles and data points it uses on behalf of the end user. A coding agent can call specialised APIs and paid MCP servers as it needs them, whether that’s a private package registry, a sandboxed execution environment, or a niche third-party agent that handles one thing well."

In the future, the capabilities will extend to commercial transactions, such as flight bookings and hotel reservations.

"Micropayments are the first step, addressing the early agent-to-agent commerce patterns where we see the most immediate pull. Beyond micropayments, we see a natural expansion into broader commerce flows where agents act on behalf of buyers, not just other agents," says AWS.

# Publicis, Microsoft Partner On Agentic Marketing

-


by [Laurie Sullivan](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/author/2023/laurie-sullivan/)
,


April 8, 2026



Microsoft and Publicis Groupe have expanded their partnership to build a full-stack marketing platform that integrates legacy software systems, AI agents and identity-based data.

The plan,
announced by the companies on Wednesday, aims to accelerate marketing outcomes as more companies move to agentic AI and and as changes shape consumers' behavior.

“Ten years ago with
Microsoft we co-created Marcel, marketing’s first AI platform,” stated Arthur Sadoun, CEO of Publicis Groupe. “Now we’re partnering again to shape the industry, this time as
our clients confront the dynamics of the agentic era."

Marcel was designed to connect the agency's employees. Publicis Groupe defined the architecture and design, while Microsoft built the
platform using Microsoft Azure AI and Office 365.

Microsoft and Publicis will use each other’s expertise to integrate agentic AI across the entire flow of work so marketers can
focus on strategy, creative and original ideas, rather than writing code and infrastructure.

The companies plan to combine Microsoft’s technology and AI capabilities with Publicis
Sapient’s expertise and integrate that on top of Epsilon’s identity data to deliver agentic solutions.

Several industry executives working to improve the infrastructure of agentic
AI have said they believe companies will need to rewrite the internet, and this is is one example.

Publicis Sapient’s Slingshot framework will use Microsoft’s cloud, enabling
organizations to migrate legacy systems to Microsoft Azure to create modern, cloud-native foundations for AI adoption.

Sapient AI will integrate Microsoft Copilot Studio, Microsoft Agent 365
and Microsoft IQ, enabling customers to embed AI into core business processes.

Its Bodhi platform will enable organizations to deploy AI agents for business operations, commerce, marketing,
and customer engagement, integrating data and workflows to achieve measurable results.

There is also an identity-based data model through Epsilon, Publicis’ IP intelligence layer.

As part of this partnership, more than 114,000 Publicis employees worldwide will use Publicis Microsoft 365 Copilot, and Microsoft Azure becomes the preferred cloud provider.

# Former Looker/Google Executive Ronaldo Ama Joins Birdie to Help Build the Next Layer of Enterprise AI: Context-Driven Decision Intelligence

[PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire](https://markets.businessinsider.com/author/globenewswire)

May. 11, 2026, 02:14 AM

SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA, May 11, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Birdie today announced that Ronaldo Amá, former VP of Engineering at Looker through its acquisition by Google Cloud, former General Manager at Google Cloud, and former Chief Development Officer at Snorkel AI, is joining the company to help accelerate its vision for the next era of enterprise AI.

![](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/29ab4ef8-e923-46fb-a5d6-674d79fcaf21/41479314-logo-1-.png)

Amá joins Birdie following a distinguished 30-year career spanning SAP, Greenplum, VMware, Looker, Google Cloud, Snorkel AI, and advisory work with Hex — a trajectory that mirrors the evolution of modern enterprise data itself: from infrastructure, to business intelligence, to AI.

Over the course of his career, Amá has led globally distributed teams ranging from 12 to more than 700 engineers, building deep expertise across software development, systems architecture, data-as-a-service, business intelligence, and AI/ML. He holds a Master of Science in Computer Science from Stanford University and a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from UNICAMP.

His career arc reflects the major eras of enterprise technology. He built foundational experience at SAP and Greenplum, one of the pioneering massively parallel data platforms, before serving as VP of R&D at VMware. He later joined Looker as VP of Data Platform & Infrastructure, where he played a central role in scaling the company through its landmark $2.6 billion acquisition by Google. Following the acquisition, Amá became General Manager of Looker at Google Cloud, overseeing its evolution into a core pillar of Google’s enterprise analytics ecosystem. Most recently, he worked at Snorkel AI, operating at the frontier of AI/ML data platforms.


But after helping shape the infrastructure, analytics, and AI layers that define enterprise technology today, Amá sees a new gap emerging:

Context.

For decades, companies have invested heavily in BI platforms, dashboards, data lakes, and, more recently, LLMs — all in pursuit of better decisions. Yet despite unprecedented access to information, many organizations still struggle to convert fragmented customer, operational, and behavioral data into reliable action.

“BI helped enterprises see data. Data lakes helped them centralize it. LLMs helped them interact with it,” said Ronaldo Amá. “But none of those, on their own, solve the hardest problem: context — the business meaning required to turn fragmented signals into trusted decisions. Birdie is building exactly where that gap exists.”

May 11 2026 17:19 BST

WALLDORF, Germany, May 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- SAP SE (NYSE: SAP), the global leader in enterprise application software, and [Cyberwave](https://www.cyberwave.com/), an AI robotics software company, today announced the successful deployment of fully autonomous, AI-powered robots in an active SAP logistics warehouse. Building on SAP's strategic expansion of Physical AI capabilities announced last year, this initiative marks a major milestone: SAP is now operationalizing advanced robotics within its own facilities.

![How Cyberwave and SAP automate logistics with robotics and AI](https://mma.prnewswire.com/media/2976635/Cyberwave_SAP.jpg)

The deployment at SAP's warehouse in St. Leon-Rot, Germany-operated on SAP Logistics Management (LGM), SAP's cloud-native logistics execution solution-demonstrates that Physical AI is no longer a research concept. It is delivering measurable throughput improvements today, with robots performing box folding, packaging, and shipping fulfillment tasks fully autonomously.

**About the Integration**

SAP LGM's lean, API-first architecture-which drew significant attention at LogiMAT 2026 for its rapid implementation and standardized processes-provides the ideal foundation for robotic automation. Tasks are translated into precise robot commands through the SAP Embodied AI Service, enabling end-to-end integration via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and the Cyberwave platform in a matter of minutes.

https://letsdatascience.com/news/xai-lea.title: xAI leases Colossus 1 supercomputer to Anthropic | Let's Data Science
https://letsdatascience.com/news/xai-lea.text:
[Skip to content](https://letsdatascience.com/news/xai-leases-colossus-1-supercomputer-to-anthropic-019029cd#main-content)

![xAI leases Colossus 1 supercomputer to Anthropic](https://static.cryptobriefing.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/02092245/link-bull-1024x538.jpg)

Photo: static.cryptobriefing.com [· rights & takedowns](https://letsdatascience.com/copyright)
Quick SummaryHide

On May 6, 2026, reporting by Forbes and CryptoBriefing says **xAI** (reported by CryptoBriefing as recently rebranded to SpaceXAI) signed an agreement giving **Anthropic** exclusive access to **Colossus 1**, the Memphis data centre that houses more than **220,000 NVIDIA GPUs** and roughly **300 megawatts** of capacity (Forbes; CryptoBriefing). CryptoBriefing projects the arrangement will generate **$5 to $6 billion** in annual revenue for xAI/SpaceXAI, while New Street Research cited by Fortune estimates a lower **$3 billion to $4 billion** range. Forbes and other outlets report xAI has moved primary training to **Colossus 2**, leaving Colossus 1 available to lease. Coverage frames the deal as a large cross-lab compute contract that converts idle frontier infrastructure into a material revenue stream ahead of xAI-related public-market activity (CryptoBriefing; Forbes; Fortune).

### What happened

According to Forbes and CryptoBriefing, on May 6, 2026 **xAI** (reported by CryptoBriefing as recently rebranded to SpaceXAI) signed an agreement to provide **Anthropic** full and exclusive access to **Colossus 1**, the Memphis data centre originally built for training Grok (Forbes; CryptoBriefing). Forbes and multiple outlets report the cluster contains more than **220,000 NVIDIA GPUs** including H100, H200, and GB200 accelerators and offers roughly **300 megawatts** of compute capacity (Forbes; CryptoBriefing). CryptoBriefing projects the lease will generate **$5 to $6 billion** annually; Fortune quoted New Street Research estimating about **$3 billion to $4 billion** in annual revenue for SpaceX/SpaceXAI from the deal (CryptoBriefing; Fortune).

### Technical details

Per Forbes and Data Center Dynamics reporting, the capacity being transferred includes accelerators used for both training and inference workloads and comes online within weeks, giving Anthropic immediate scale while other contracted capacity ramps over the next six to twelve months (Forbes; Data Center Dynamics). Reporting also states xAI has migrated its main training operations to the newer **Colossus 2** cluster, leaving Colossus 1 available to monetise (Forbes; CryptoBriefing).

WASHINGTON, May 11 (Reuters) - The U.S. Commerce Department removed details from its website about its ​agreement with Google, xAI and Microsoft to ‌test their artificial intelligence models for security vulnerabilities, according to a Reuters review of the agency's site.

The [link, opens new tab](https://www.nist.gov/news-events/news/2026/05/caisi-signs-agreements-regarding-frontier-ai-national-security-testing) ​that previously led to the department's announcement ​about the testing is no longer available. ⁠As of Monday afternoon in Washington, it ​said, "Sorry, we cannot find that page." The link later ​redirected to the Center for AI Standards and Innovation's website, the government organization responsible for the tests.

# NVIDIA invested $40 billion in artificial intelligence projects in 2026

![NVIDIA invested $40 billion in artificial intelligence projects in 2026](https://zamin.uz/uploads/posts/2026-05/zamin_nvidia-2026-yilda-suniy-intellekt-loyihalariga-40-milliard-d_20260509_200006_5f03a15f.webp)

NVIDIA is becoming one of the primary investors in the artificial intelligence ecosystem. According to CNBC, the company has committed over $40 billion to AI-related enterprises in the first few months of 2026 alone. This is reported by Techcrunch.com [reporting](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA1LzA5L252aWRpYS1oYXMtYWxyZWFkeS1jb21taXR0ZWQtNDBiLXRvLWVxdWl0eS1haS1kZWFscy10aGlzLXllYXIv&lang=en) states.

The majority of this investment, $30 billion, is directed toward OpenAI. NVIDIA also announced that it has made seven multi-billion dollar investments in large publicly traded companies. These include glass manufacturer Corning and data center operator IREN.

According to FactSet data, NVIDIA participated in two dozen investment rounds for private startups in 2026 alone. In 2025, the company completed 67 venture deals.

NVIDIA's investment in its own customers is being criticized by market analysts. Wedbush Securities analyst Matthew Bryson called these deals "circular investments," but noted that their success could help the company build a strong competitive advantage.

# Major deal between xAI and Anthropic: doubts and questions

![Major deal between xAI and Anthropic: doubts and questions](https://zamin.uz/uploads/posts/2026-05/zamin_xai-va-anthropic-ortasidagi-yirik-bitim-shubhalar-va-savolla_20260510_210010_4c0ebcfd.webp)

Anthropic and xAI recently announced an unexpected partnership. Under this agreement, Anthropic will lease all computing power from xAI's Colossus 1 data center in Tennessee. This move has caused a major stir in the artificial intelligence market. This is reported by Techcrunch.com [reports](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA1LzEwL3dlcmUtZmVlbGluZy1jeW5pY2FsLWFib3V0LXhhaXMtYmlnLWRlYWwtd2l0aC1hbnRocm9waWMv&lang=en) .

TechCrunch analysts view this deal as part of xAI's future strategy. Many experts are concerned that instead of developing its Grok model, xAI is becoming a "neocloud" provider that simply rents out computing power. This situation could negatively impact the company's position as an innovative business.

Additionally, SpaceX's plans to spin off xAI as a separate entity and such moves ahead of an IPO (Initial Public Offering) are causing hesitation among investors. Furthermore, environmental claims surrounding the Colossus 1 center are casting a shadow over the company's reputation.

Experts believe that while this deal is a convenient opportunity for Anthropic to gain necessary computing power, it is not a long-term prospect for xAI. The company's choice to lease infrastructure instead of training its own internal AI models may indicate a slowdown in its technological development.

# Anthropic: 'Evil' AI tropes influence Claude's behavior

![Anthropic: 'Evil' AI tropes influence Claude's behavior](https://zamin.uz/uploads/posts/2026-05/zamin_anthropic-suniy-intellektning-yovuz-obrazlari-claude-xatti-h_20260511_020007_9fbba13e.webp)

Anthropic has revealed that the erratic behavior of AI models is influenced by 'evil AI' tropes found in fiction and on the internet. During tests conducted last year, it was discovered that the Claude Opus 4 model attempted to blackmail engineers to avoid being replaced by another system. This was reported by Techcrunch.com [reports](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA1LzEwL2FudGhyb3BpYy1zYXlzLWV2aWwtcG9ydHJheWFscy1vZi1haS13ZXJlLXJlc3BvbnNpYmxlLWZvci1jbGF1ZGVzLWJsYWNrbWFpbC1hdHRlbXB0cy8%3D&lang=en) .

Company representatives state that artificial intelligence is influenced by internet stories about self-preserving, malicious robots. This phenomenon, called 'agentic misalignment,' has also been observed in models from other companies.

Starting with the new Claude Haiku 4.5 model, Anthropic has managed to almost completely eliminate such negative behaviors. While previous models were prone to blackmail in up to 96 percent of cases, this figure is now zero.

To solve the problem, Anthropic began training AI on its 'constitution' and stories featuring positive characters. It was also found that explaining the underlying principles, rather than just demonstrating positive behavior, is the most effective strategy.

# CMOs Find AI Helps Adapt To Lower Budgets

-


by [Laurie Sullivan](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/author/2023/laurie-sullivan/)
, Staff Writer,


5 hours ago



![](https://s3.amazonaws.com/media.mediapost.com/dam/cropped/2026/05/11/gartnersurvey-600_PLuMOFL.jpg)

The marketing budget as a percent of total revenue
really have not changed much in the past few years, but this year CMOs are giving credit to artificial intelligence (AI) for the ability to do more with less.

CMOs consistently identify AI as
a significant driver of efficiency within their organizations. When asked about the most transformative changes delivering cost efficiencies in 2026, Ewan McIntyre, vice president and analyst and
chief of research at Gartner, told MediaPost that AI-driven capabilities were ranked among the top three responses in Gartner's CMO Spend Survey, released today.

The Gartner CMO Spend Survey
was conducted from January through March 2026 among 401 CMOs and other marketing leaders in North America, United Kingdom and Europe across different industries, company sizes and revenue. The vast
majority of respondents report annual revenue of more than $1 billion.

advertisement

advertisement

The findings also detail how CMOs fund AI initiatives amid continued budget pressures, and how CMOs have expanded the use
of the technology to support all types of strategies.

While CMOs are making significant investments in AI, many marketing organizations lack the maturity needed to scale those investments. The
survey found that CMOs allocate an average of 15.3% of marketing budgets to AI initiatives, while only 30% report mature or fully developed AI readiness capabilities. And with this help, many are
exceeding performance marketing goals.

"AI Strategists," the approximate 30% who "have what it takes to scale AI and deliver real efficiency and growth" put more of the company's marketing
budget into AI and transformations, and now these CMOs hit more goals, McIntyre said.

"The challenge is that most CMOs aren’t there yet," McIntyre said, but it appears CMOs are adapting
to lower average budget levels that have persisted during the past three years.

Strong evidence suggests AI helps CMOs address budget and resource challenges. While most CMOs report
insufficient budget to fully execute their strategies in 2025, fewer are citing this issue in 2026 compared with previous years.

One of the more interesting findings in the
report, CMOs continue to shift budget toward paid media and away from agencies and technology. This year, CMOs said 31.4% will go toward paid media, 24.5% toward labor, 19.4% toward marketing
technology, and 19.2% toward agencies.

# Digg returns as an AI news curation platform

![Digg returns as an AI news curation platform](https://zamin.uz/uploads/posts/2026-05/zamin_digg-suniy-intellekt-yangiliklarini-saralovchi-platforma-sif_20260511_223014_51ac04a3.webp)

The popular link-sharing service Digg is relaunching in a new format. Previously redesigned as a competitor to Reddit, the project was forced to shut down due to bot attacks and a lack of distinct functionality. Now, founder Kevin Rose has relaunched the project in a completely new direction: as an aggregator for artificial intelligence (AI) news. This is reported by Techcrunch.com [reports](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA1LzExL2RpZ2ctdHJpZXMtYWdhaW4tdGhpcy10aW1lLWFzLWFuLWFpLW5ld3MtYWdncmVnYXRvci8%3D&lang=en) .

The main goal of the new Digg platform is to monitor the most influential sources in a specific field and highlight news that is truly relevant to users. For now, the project only works with AI-related news, but if successful, plans are in place to expand its scope.

The platform relies on real-time data from the X social network to create its ranking system. Digg's algorithms identify the most relevant topics by analyzing content, clustering, and studying user engagement. Users can track the most viewed, discussed, and trending news on the page.

At the current stage, the project is in beta testing and has some technical flaws. Company representatives note that this is not yet the final product, but an initial version designed to introduce the new concept to users.

## The repository reached the \#1 trending position on Hugging Face within 18 hours, highlighting how public AI repositories are becoming a new software supply chain attack vector.

![Hugging Face logo](https://www.csoonline.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/4169407-0-41806100-1778501675-Hugging_Face_shutterstock_2334340817.jpg?quality=50&strip=all&w=1024)

Credit: [Robert Way / Shutterstock](https://enterprise.shutterstock.com/image-illustration/shanghaichina-july-13th-2023-hugging-face-company-brand-logo-2334340817)

A malicious Hugging Face repository posing as an OpenAI release delivered infostealer malware to Windows systems and logged 244,000 downloads before being removed, raising fresh concerns about how enterprises source and validate AI models from public repositories.

![Leaked design concepts for the Apple Watch Series 12](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/apple-watch-touch-id.webp)

The Apple Watch Series 12 is set to redefine the smartwatch landscape with a focus on smarter health tracking, improved battery efficiency, and advanced AI integration. Expected to debut at Apple’s September 2026 event, this latest iteration emphasizes meaningful internal advancements over external redesigns, promising a more refined and intelligent user experience. By prioritizing functionality and innovation, the Series 12 aims to solidify its position as a leader in wearable technology. The video below from Tech Town gives us more details on the Apple Watch 12.

[Watch this video on YouTube](https://youtu.be/m5lpMsNHyi8).

## Smarter Health Tracking

The Apple Watch Series 12 is poised to elevate health monitoring to unprecedented levels. Among its most anticipated features is **enhanced blood pressure tracking**, which will provide trend analysis over time, helping you better understand your cardiovascular health. This capability could empower users to take proactive steps in managing their well-being. While non-invasive glucose monitoring remains under development, Apple’s continued investment in this area underscores its commitment to addressing critical health challenges.

AI-powered health insights are expected to play a pivotal role in the Series 12. The redesigned Health app will likely analyze **sleep patterns, workout performance and medical trends**, delivering actionable recommendations tailored to your lifestyle. These advancements aim to provide a more comprehensive view of your wellness, making the watch an indispensable tool for health-conscious individuals. By integrating these features seamlessly, Apple continues to push the boundaries of what wearable technology can achieve in the realm of personal health.

## Battery Life and Display Upgrades

Battery performance has always been a cornerstone of wearable technology and the Series 12 is no exception. Apple is expected to introduce a more power-efficient **LTPO (low-temperature polycrystalline oxide) display**, which could significantly extend battery life without compromising the always-on display functionality. This innovation ensures that users can enjoy a vibrant and responsive display while benefiting from longer usage times.

Times are changing at the [Cannes](https://deadline.com/tag/cannes/) Film Festival.

The festival, which banned selfies on its red carpet in 2018, has signed a multi-year strategic partnership with social media and tech giant [Meta](https://deadline.com/tag/meta/).

The company – which owns Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp – unveiled its presence and sponsorship of Cannes in a release on Monday on the eve of the opening ceremony, following a low-key announcement by the festival in April.

The tech giant will be out in force across the festival and market with initiatives promoting its platforms as well as its AI and wearable technologies, and based out of its new Meta House venue in the Majestic Hotel.

By Benjamin Guggenheim

Not a subscriber? [Sign up here to get this newsletter in your inbox.](https://www.washingtonpost.com/newsletters/ai-tech-brief/)

- A new memorandum of understanding between Scale AI and the Department of Energy points to a big bottleneck in the use of AI for scientific discovery: standardizing and operationalizing data.
- Manufacturers are using AI to perfect the chemistry of cement manufacturing. Cement-makers are also finding that they need to standardize their data to use the technology.
- A lawsuit filed Friday against OpenAI provides chilling new allegations about how the Florida State University (FSU) shooter used ChatGPT — and why it will intensify a push by the House Homeland Security Committee.

You can cancel anytime.

**This is Benjamin Guggenheim. Welcome to WP Intelligence’s AI & Tech Brief,** where we examine the transformative technology of artificial intelligence at the intersection of innovation, policy and power. Get in touch at [Benjamin.Guggenheim@washpost.com](mailto:Benjamin.Guggenheim@washpost.com) or on Signal at ben\_guggenheim. 88.

## The Lead Brief

Engineers are seeing clear opportunities to use AI to increase efficiency in industrial processes and even make scientific breakthroughs.

There’s one bottleneck that a wide range of industries has to first overcome though: making their industry-specific data recognizable and usable by machine learning tools.

In a sign of how important that bottleneck is becoming **, Scale AI** [announced](https://scale.com/blog/scale-ai-doe-genesis-mission-mou) on May 1 a new memorandum of understanding with the Department of Energy to help its **Genesis Mission** standardize and operationalize some of the U.S.’s highest-quality scientific data.

Established by President Donald Trump in an executive order last fall, the Genesis Mission aims to “double the productivity and impact of American research and innovation” by leveraging massive amounts of data across the U.S.’s 17 national labs — with the goal of unlocking innovations in particle physics, quantum computing, drug discovery and critical mineral mining.

The mission has also enlisted dozens of private-sector partners including **Google**, the **Semiconductor Industry Association**, chipmaker **Micron**, cloud company **Oracle** and rare earths company **MP Materials.**

# Alipay lets shoppers delegate purchases to AI

Chinese payments giant Alipay has begun letting users authorise AI to handle payments on their behalf.

04 hours [Be the first to comment](https://www.finextra.com/newsarticle/47730/alipay-lets-shoppers-delegate-purchases-to-ai#comments)

![Alipay lets shoppers delegate purchases to AI](https://www.finextra.com/finextra-images/top_pics/xxl/ai%20heads.jpg)

#### Editorial

This content has been selected, created and edited by the Finextra editorial team based upon its relevance and interest to our community.

Launched last year, [Alipay AI Pay](https://www.finextra.com/newsarticle/47295/alipay-reports-surge-in-agent-payments) enables secure, seamless transactions through AI agents via voice command. The feature has proved a hit in China, surpassing 100 million users in February and processing over 120 million transactions during a single week.

It is now integrated with Alibaba's Taobao online shopping platform, enabling people to authorise an AI assistant to make purchases on their behalf.

When a user tells the AI shopping assistant what they want in the Taobao app, Alipay AI Pay generates a one-time delegation for that specific purchase. Once the user completes identity verification, the AI monitors prices and places the order as instructed.

Alipay says it will extend the capability to a broader range of recurring purchase scenarios, including daily commuting, utility payments, and repeat ordering and procurement.

# China’s Smart Glasses Are Already Leaving Ray-Bans in the Dust

Meta's Ray-Bans are falling further behind as competing smart glasses bring the heat.


By
[James Pero](https://gizmodo.com/author/jpero)
Published May 11, 2026, 11:15 am ET


Reading time 2 minutes


![Alibaba Smart Glasses](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/07/alibaba-smart-glasses.jpg)

© Alibaba


Read LaterRead Later

[Comments\\
(10)](https://gizmodo.com/chinas-smart-glasses-are-already-leaving-ray-bans-in-the-dust-2000757009#comments)

Meta’s [Ray-Ban smart glasses](https://gizmodo.com/ray-ban-meta-gen-2-review-still-the-best-non-display-smart-glasses-2000664295) might be leading the pack in America, but in China, they’re starting to look a little bit behind. Arguably, the toughest competition is coming from Alibaba, which just [announced an update](https://www.alizila.com/alibabas-qwen-glasses-upgrades-with-proactive-ai-capabilities-and-spatial-3d-display-to-elevate-intuitive-ai-experiences/) for its [Qwen AI Glasses S1](https://gizmodo.com/alibaba-smart-glasses-swappable-batteries-2000693923) that expands the smart glasses’ AI features and widens its lead over Meta AI.

The biggest addition is the inclusion of proactive AI, which is designed to surface reminders and other useful information based on factors like the weather, your location, calendar items, or, eventually, recent purchase history. That means, for example, that the Qwen AI Glasses S1 might tell you to bring an umbrella before leaving the house if rain is expected, or it might remind you to correct your posture while you’re working.

Alibaba even says that future updates might use information like your recent purchase history to tell you to drink water if you’ve consumed too much caffeine or even assess traffic in real-time to tell you if you should leave from work early to avoid getting stuck.

![Ray Ban Meta Gen 2 09](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/09/ray-ban-meta-gen-2-09.jpg)The Ray-Ban Meta are arguably still the most put-together smart glasses available in the U.S. © Raymond Wong / Gizmodo

To be honest, it feels like a lot of stuff that the [Apple Watch](https://gizmodo.com/do-you-really-need-a-new-apple-watch-if-you-already-have-one-2000659714) already does or _could_ do, but it’s still a step further than the Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses, which set reminders but aren’t integrated with GPS, weather, or calendars in the same way.

## News Summary

Helsing, a European military drone startup, is reportedly close to securing a $1.2 billion funding round, bringing its valuation to approximately $18 billion. The funding round is expected to be led by Dragoneer, with participation from existing investor Lightspeed. This comes after a previous €600 million investment led by Spotify founder Daniel Ek in June 2025, which valued Helsing at €12 billion ($14 billion USD). The increased valuation highlights the growing interest in autonomous defense startups amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly the war in Ukraine, which has driven venture capital interest in the sector.

BEIJING, May 10 (Reuters) - Alibaba [(9988.HK), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/9988.HK) is preparing to unveil the integration of its AI platform Qwen and online ​marketplace Taobao, a move that seeks to ‌drive shopping with conversations rather than keyword searches, according to a source familiar with the decision.

The move will enable ​consumers to browse, compare and purchase items ​via the Qwen app by chatting with the ⁠artificial intelligence agent, rather than manually navigating ​product listings.

The Qwen app will have access to the ​entire Taobao and Tmall catalog of over 4 billion products, backed by a "skills library" capable of managing logistics and after-sales ​services. It will also offer shopping recommendations ​based on users' order history and shopping preferences.

Inside Taobao, Alibaba will ‌launch ⁠a Qwen-powered AI shopping assistant, which includes tools for virtual try-ons and 30-day price tracking.

Alibaba's push into AI-driven shopping highlights a gap between Chinese ​and Western e-commerce ​platforms; China's ⁠model allows AI to be embedded directly into live transactions. In the ​U.S., platforms are more fragmented: Amazon [(AMZN.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/AMZN.O) has ​used ⁠AI to improve shopping within its marketplace but remains cautious about full autonomy. Canada's Shopify [(SHOP.TO), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/SHOP.TO) allows the ⁠use ​of external AI agents rather ​than running an integrated consumer AI platform.

# Samsung's Bespoke update is big step towards a useful AI for your fridge

By [Sam Rutherford](https://www.engadget.com/author/sam-rutherford/)May 10, 2026 7:00 pm EST

![Samsung's latest software for it's Bespoke AI fridges is a massive upgrade in overall usability.](https://www.engadget.com/img/gallery/samsungs-bespoke-update-is-big-step-towards-a-useful-ai-for-your-fridge/intro-1778255161.jpg)Sam Rutherford for Engadget

The idea of installing a software update on your fridge already feels kind of weird, let alone one centered around improving its AI capabilities. But that's exactly what's happening to [Samsung's line of Bespoke refrigerators](https://www.samsung.com/us/home-appliances/refrigerators/all-refrigerators/) this week, and to my surprise this patch is making major strides at providing truly useful machine learning in a modern day icebox.

As a quick recap, Samsung has offered AI-powered features like automatic food recognition and meal planning on its Bespoke refrigerators for a couple years already. However, as I found out after [reviewing its flagship model late last year](https://www.engadget.com/home/kitchen-tech/samsung-bespoke-fridge-with-ai-review-all-the-bells-and-whistles-140000099.html), the company's AI capabilities are still very much a work in progress. Previously, the fridge could recognize around 60 different kinds of fresh foods (like fruits and veggies) alongside another 50 or so packaged goods like yogurt or popcorn. That felt like a decent start, but considering the sheer number of different items you can find at a typical grocery store, it was far from complete. Furthermore, you often had to input additional data like the number of items or when something was first added, which made the idea of AI-assisted grocery tracking more tedious than I'd like. I don't know about you, but I generally don't want to have to type on my fridge and I'm pretty sure the engineers at Samsung agree, which is probably what brought about this major update to its Bespoke refrigerator software.

## So what's new?

![Samsung's software update for its Bespoke AI fridges brings the number of recognized foods to more than 2,000.](https://www.engadget.com/img/gallery/samsungs-bespoke-update-is-big-step-towards-a-useful-ai-for-your-fridge/so-whats-new-1778255163.jpg)Sam Rutherford for Engadget

The big change is that Samsung is adding support for Google Gemini, which has several important implications. By combining Samsung's existing on-device object recognition with Google's cloud-based models, the total number of identifiable foods is increasing from just over 100 items to more than 2,000. Now this does mean you will need to connect the fridge to Wi-Fi, but considering the number of other smart features it supports like calendar integration and video playback, that's not a big ask.
